mission.title: Install and Configure Langflow
mission.description: Langflow is a powerful platform for building and deploying AI-powered agents and workflows, providing a visual authoring experience and built-in API servers. It is ideal for developers looking to integrate AI workflows into applications without managing complex dependencies. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential resource overhead.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the Langflow CLI using pip to manage your Langflow application.

```bash

pip install langflow==1.8.0

```

> Ensure you have Python 3.10-3.13 installed.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Create a GKE cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create langflow-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig to access the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials langflow-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command sets up your local kubeconfig.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Deploy Langflow
steps.description:
Deploy Langflow to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add langflow https://charts.langflow.org

helm install langflow langflow/langflow --version 1.8.0 --namespace langflow --create-namespace

```

> Add the Langflow Helm repository.
> Deploy Langflow in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your Langflow deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment langflow --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 --namespace langflow

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Set up autoscaling based on CPU usage.
> Ensure you have a monitoring configuration file ready.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Langflow
uninstall.description:
Remove the Langflow deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data stored in the cluster.

```bash

helm uninstall langflow --namespace langflow

gcloud container clusters delete langflow-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command removes the Langflow release.
> This command deletes the entire cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Langflow
upgrade.description:
Backup your current configuration, upgrade the deployment, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all --namespace langflow > backup-langflow.txt

helm upgrade langflow langflow/langflow --version 1.8.0 --namespace langflow

kubectl get pods --namespace langflow

```

> Backup current resources.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or there may be network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure your cluster has internet access.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be failing to start due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace langflow' for details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resize existing nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is running and check the service configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved the visual builder interface.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' when running 'kubectl get pods --namespace langflow'. All pods should be in the 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
